text: �Crisis Response: Uses logic and calm to survive. Wants to make sure the Mall Rats are safe, then figure out what’s going on. �Location: Around the mall. MALL RATS ▶ Bruce (he/him) - Maintenance �Young. Coveralls and tool-belt. �Easy to talk to. �Did not like Billy (p. 6). �Crisis Response: Survive at all costs, uses knowledge of mall. �Location: Maintenance (p. 14). ▶ Emmy (she/her) - Armorer �Trans, spiked chainmail over flannel, hair in a bun. �Crisis Response: To protect her shop (her magnum opus). �Location: Bartering weapons for resources from Emmy’s Swords & Stuff (p. 18). ▶ Kurt (he/him) - Assistant Manager �Older, soft spoken, long hair. �Pretzel stand’s last employee. �Rumors are true: Kurt has in fact, “seen some shit.” �Crisis Response: Takes decisive action. Thinks the factions are as dangerous as the monster. �Location: Searching for supplies. Fortifying Stan’s Pretzel Stand (p. 20). ▶ Logan (he/him) - DMV Employee �White button down, black tie and pants, thick glasses. �Knows every conspiracy theory. �Believes that the mysterious Bureau is involved in some way in what has been going on. �Crisis Response: “You can’t trust anyone if it can look like anything!” �Location:DMV (p. 15). Out seeking those who desire truth. Whether benefiting from the chaos, trying to solve the problem, accusing others or just trying to survive, these NPCs are not aligned with any faction. UNAFFILIATED NPCS DYING IN THE MALL Taking Critical Damage or experiencing extreme pain in the mall causes PCs and NPCs to pierce the veil. As the portal and its spores lose hold, the person briefly “resyncs” and glimpses the real world. While risky and hard to replicate, maintaining extreme levels of damage will allow a PC to break through the Barrier (p. 12), in exchange for permanent bodily damage. REPLACED To determine if an NPC has been REPLACED: ▶Roll a d10 against the DOOM level and make a CTRL save. �If the result is equal or under AND they fail a CTRL save, they have been REPLACED. As the adventure progresses, NPCs’ CTRL may decrease. QUICK REFERENCE Population: Approximately 100 employees and customers trapped in the mall at the start of this module. Baseline Stats: Unless otherwise noted, Ability Scores are 10 (STR, DEX, CTRL) and 3 HP. For advice on modifying stats, see Investigators: Creating Monsters. Weapons: Unarmed strikes deal d4, and most weapons deal d6. For more, see Investigators: Equipment List. [08] Unaffiliated NPCS & Mechanics // Pain unclouds one’s vision. CHILDREN OF AMMON // Wet, gooey, and gross. Frame their appearances like the PCs are in a film. [09] MONSTER TABLE - 3d8 Use this table to inspire the monsters’ different iterations. It has no standard anatomy and can take on many forms before replacing the people around it. MULTIPLE TWISTED FACES ERUPTING SPINE FUNGAL STALKS TUMOROUS GROWTHS WINGS OF BONE SKINLESS DISTENDING BISECTED PULSING JAGGED OPENINGS IMPOSSIBLE ANGLES WHIPPING TUBES MULTI-HINGED JAW FLESH SPIDER CLAWS SPURS OF BONE APPENDAGES EMERGING MELTING UNFOLDING PREHENSILE LIMBS SPEWING SPORES BLOOMING CRAB-LIKE WRITHING MASS To bring all flesh into itself. To throw open the portal beneath the mall. CHILDREN OF AMMON DRIVE When consumed and replicated, the victim becomes REPLACED. By all outward appearance they seem perfectly normal, but act now as a Child of Ammon. THE REPLACED The only sure way to know if someone has been REPLACED is to kill them. Near bodily death, a Child of Ammon will attempt to save a piece of itself. THE FLESH IS WEAK Destroying the portal will neutralize the spores within the mall’s denizens, but it will not eradicate The Children of Ammon that are already on the loose. IF EVEN A CELL GET’S OUT! It prefers to integrate and assimilate out of sight, dragging fresh or living victims away (often into the vents). THIS FLESH WAS ONCE YOURS ▶HP: 3-5 (flesh quantity/raw size) ▶Armor: +1-2 (bone shards/fungus) ▶STR: 12+ (size) ▶DEX: 10+ (size and number of limbs) ▶CTRL: 13 ▶Damage: d6 - d12 (number of appendages, size, bones, fungus). Metamorphosis: Can change forms in moments, sometimes even breaking off into multiple unique instances. Anointed Blood: Spray a mix of blood, and spores. Target takes a Whisper Card (p. 37), witnesses take d4 Stress. Critical Damage: Fleshy tendrils pierce the victim’s flesh, worming their way between muscle and bone (D4 damage to DEX). STATS Locations // The mall is massive. It is a necropolis to failing capitalism. [11] LOCATIONS GENERAL FEATURES ▶Stores have glass facades. ▶Storefront security gates that can be lowered and locked. ▶Store Keys: �At least one store employee has a key. �Maintenance (p. 14) has a copy of each individual key. �Security (p. 17) is supposed to have a copy of each key. �Manager’s Office (p. 19) has a master key. (false rumor) ▶Most store have rear doors that connect to the access hallways. ▶ Each store has speakers connected to Security (p. 17). ▶ Vents, Access Hallways and Parking Garages connect to all 3 floors. ACCESS HALLWAYS (ALL LEVELS) ▶Not fully included on the map, they spider-web throughout the mall and connect to most stores. ▶Primary access to Loading Docks (p. 14), Break Room (p. 16) and between levels. ▶A maintenance stairwell connects all floors to the Basement (p. 21). ▶Access to long-abandoned rooms now used as storage. �One stores oxygen tanks from a failed “spa” that took investor money and ran. �Others contain supplies from closed shops (at first). A hastily set up stage and a “Save The Mall” banner, adjacent to Skate World Roller Rink(p. 15). ▶A 1973 Oldsmobile Delta 88, parked at the stage’s base. DOOM 1-2 ▶7pm: Visitors gather for the raffle Mr. Neil(p. 6) gives a speech. ▶It is cut short by flashing of orange lights followed by an loud crash. ▶A Child of Ammon erupts from behind the stage, attacking and killing an NPC. One option is to have a NPC that is connected to the players (p. 34). ▶The crowd devolves into chaos as Maggie(p. 6) tries to coordinate an escape. 1. CONTEST
